theme: Verdancourt, the Greening Garth — a terraced herb-garden town built around the Greenchord, a buried root-vein that "hums in the key of growing things." In Cantorne's sung world, plants are slow notes: a seed is a held breath, a ripe herb a sustained tone, a brewed potion a chord struck once and drunk. Verdancourt's wardens are the Sustainers, who keep the long green note true; the Offnote rots it into Blight (withered crops, sour brews). Aesthetic: trellised allotment terraces, ringing watering troughs, a humming brew-cauldron that "resolves" reagents into chords. Potions are bottled chords — drinking one briefly tunes your body sharper (a stat boost) or steadies it (restore).
summary: Today Farming is a 4-seed afterthought (potato L1, cabbage L7, sweetcorn L20, watermelon L47) with exactly TWO farming_patch tiles on the whole 300x300 map and no herb-growing at all — every grimy herb comes from mob drops, so the grow side of the loop is dead. Herblore is a 4-step ladder (clean guam L3 → marrentill L5 → ranarr L25 → irit L40; potions attack L3 / defence L9 / prayer L38 / super-attack L45) with a single secondary in existence (eye_of_newt) and — critically — combat potions are FLAVOR-ONLY (server consume handler restores nothing but prayer; attack/defence/super do nothing). The expansion adds: (1) Verdancourt, a dedicated herb-garden hub with a dense clustered allotment so players barely walk; (2) a full herb-seed tier ladder grown on farming_patch via the existing rake→plant→harvest intents (zero engine change, herb seeds slot into recipes.json.seeds with the grimy herb as "produce"); (3) clean + secondary tiers and a real potion ladder reaching L92 (extreme/sustain potions); (4) ONE honest engine hook — a data-driven `boost`/`heal` field on the consume intent, mirroring the existing `restoresPrayer` precedent — so combat potions finally DO something and become the combat sink the brief wants; (5) reward sinks at the Sustainer's Reward Trough (compost/auto-loop QoL, a herb-sack, a gardener outfit set with an XP bonus). All numbers sit on the existing Farming/Herblore curves.

NEW intent 'farm-compost' (server/intent-produce.ts): near compost_bin (<=2), Farming L20 for compost / L50 for supercompost, consumes withered-crop or N basic crops, grants 1 compost(super), small xp. Mirrors farm-rake structure.

NEW intent 'farm-apply-compost' OR extend farm-plant payload with {compost?:itemId}: on raked patch before/at planting, consume 1 compost -> patch flagged so farm-harvest yields fixed 5 (compost) and herb patches get +1 yield (supercompost). Stored on the existing FarmPatch state object as compost:'compost'|'supercompost'.

ENGINE HOOK on existing 'consume' intent: read item.boost/heal/restoreStats/restoreEnergy from items.json and apply transient state.statBoosts; expand DRINKABLE allow-list to include all new potion ids.
newObjectsOrMechanics: OBJECT type 'farming_patch' (EXISTS) — reuse as-is for allotment crops AND herb patches; no schema change. Verdancourt places a dense cluster of these. State model unchanged (rake/plant/harvest already validate seed+level server-side).

OBJECT type 'compost_bin' — new world object, action 'Compost'. Server: a new intent 'farm-compost' that, when standing <=2 tiles, consumes 1 of {withered_crop or 15 of a basic crop} and grants 1 compost (Farming L20) / supercompost (L50). Mirrors the make pattern; small Farming xp. Add to objects.json (blocks:false).

OBJECT type 'limpwurt_plant' — gather object (GATHER_REQS: skill Herblore? no — keep as Farming gather, tool none) yielding limpwurt_root. Reuses the generic 'gather' intent; add a GATHER_REQS entry {skill:'Farming'} and an object def. Level-gate via object roll.
OBJECT type 'white_berry_bush' — gather object yielding white_berries (Farming gather, no tool).
OBJECT type 'snape_grass' — gather object yielding snape_grass (no tool).

OBJECT type 'brew_cauldron' — FLAVOR/station object (action 'Brew'). NOTE: current potion recipes are stationless via the generic 'make' intent, so brewing works WITHOUT this. If the integrator wants potions gated to Verdancourt, add 'potion' to the make-intent stationReq switch (server/intents.ts ~line 389) mapping to 'brew_cauldron'; otherwise place it as decoration. Recommend stationless to preserve current behaviour.

MECHANIC (ENGINE HOOK, the one real addition): extend the existing 'consume' intent (server/intent-produce.ts ~line 684) to read data-driven consumable effects from items.json, exactly mirroring the existing restoresPrayer path. Add optional item keys: `boost`: { skill: SkillName, amount: number, capPct?: number }, `restoreStats`: SkillName[] (restore lowered combat levels to base), `restoreEnergy`: number, `heal`: number. Server applies a transient boost to the player's effective combat level used by the swing path. Because combat already trusts client-sent `eff`/`bonus`/`maxHit` from the swing intent (EFFECTS.md §3.1) with the SERVER re-deriving from owned state, the integrator stores boosts in a new transient state field (e.g. state.statBoosts: {skill, amount, until}[]) read where skillLevel() feeds combat. This is the SAME shape/precedent as prayerPoints+restoresPrayer and activePrayers — no new combat math, just an additive level delta. Keep DRINKABLE allow-list and expand it to the new potion ids.

MECHANIC: herb growing needs NO new code — herb seeds are added to recipes.json.seeds with the grimy herb as `produce`. The existing farm-plant/farm-harvest loop validates Farming level, consumes seed, grants grimy-herb produce + harvestXp. Cleaning + brewing then ride the existing 'clean|' and 'potion|' make recipes.
newNpcs: warden_brisa — Verdancourt Seed Trough keeper (shop: all herb seeds + allotment seeds + compost + rake/dibber/trowel)
master_sustainer_oak — Herblore/Farming trainer & lore (the Greenchord, the Blight); gives skill tips and gates the Resonant Terrace
apothecary_vell — Verdancourt Apothecary (shop: vial_of_water, eye_of_newt, secondaries limpwurt/white_berries/snape_grass; buys grimy herbs)
keeper_thistle — Sustainer Reward Trough vendor (spends Sustainer points: herb_sack, gardener outfit, resonant_dibber, supercompost, truechord_seed)
blight_warden_mara — quest/lore NPC who fights crop Blight; hands out antiblight_tonic recipe and Blight herb-seed drops
trainingLayout: Verdancourt is a 3-terrace garden so a player can rake/plant/harvest/clean/brew without leaving a ~10-tile core — directly answering the 'barely walk' priority. TERRACE 1 (Allotment Garth, lower): a 4x4 BLOCK of 16 farming_patch tiles in a tight grid with 1-tile walkways, flanked by Warden Brisa's Seed Trough and a water trough (refill vials) and 2 compost_bins — this is the food/low farming grind. TERRACE 2 (Herb Rows, middle): TWO rows of 8 farming_patch tiles (16 total) reserved for herb seeds, with Apothecary Vell's stall and a brew_cauldron between the rows, plus a cluster of 3 limpwurt_plant + 2 white_berry_bush + 3 snape_grass gather nodes against the wall so secondaries are 2 tiles from the cauldron — the core grow+clean+brew loop lives entirely here. TERRACE 3 (Resonant Terrace, upper, Herblore 82 / Farming 85 gated by Master Sustainer Oak): a 3x3 = 9 farming_patch cluster for truechord_seed plus the Sustainer Reward Trough and 2 extra compost_bins. Total: 41 farming_patch tiles (vs 2 today), 8 secondary gather nodes, all within Verdancourt. A bank chest sits at the junction of all three terraces so banking is ≤4 tiles from any patch.
rewards: SUSTAINER POINTS earned per herb harvested / potion brewed (server grants alongside xp, like a token currency) spent at Keeper Thistle's Reward Trough: (1) herb_sack (4000pts) — holds 30 of each grimy herb, kills banking trips; (2) Sustainer outfit set — gardeners_hat/top/legs/boots (2000pts each), full set = +5% Farming xp; (3) resonant_dibber (8000pts) — top farming tool, chance to not consume seed; (4) supercompost & bulk compost (gold + withered-crop sink); (5) truechord_seed (rare, 6000pts) — the only reliable route to the L85 herb, a deep endgame sink. GOLD/MATERIAL SINKS: high-tier seeds (cadantine 100g, dwarf_weed 140g, truechord 260g) and resonant_dust (120g, also consumes cross-skill ringing-ore shards) drain the economy; extreme potions cost a super-potion + resonant_dust each, so the best combat consumables are a recurring sink. Combat sink loop: PvE/boss players burn strength/super-strength/ranging/extreme/truechord_draught per trip, and super_restore/prayer_renewal to sustain — making Herblore the supply spine for all combat.
mapAreaProposal: Place Verdancourt in a temperate, well-watered lowland in the SOUTH-CENTRAL map between Port Brackwater (~105,196) and Gullswreck (~94,262) — a green river-valley region the existing towns frame but leave empty. Footprint ~16x18 tiles for the 3 stacked terraces (Allotment Garth lower-south, Herb Rows middle, Resonant Terrace upper-north against a low ridge where the Greenchord root-vein surfaces). Roughly the (110-128, 215-235) band — riverbank biome, fed by a stream that justifies the water troughs. Final coords set at integration; just keep it in the southern fertile lowland, not near the frost/mining north.
balanceNotes: XP curve fit: cleaning xp scales smoothly from the existing guam 2.5 → irit 8.8, continuing harralander 27.5? — CORRECTION for the curve: clean xp should follow the existing gentle ramp, so use harralander 12.5, toadflax 15, avantoe 24.5, kwuarm 28, cadantine 30, dwarf_weed 33.5, truechord 38 (the higher numbers in the recipe list above are the brew xp, not clean — integrator: clean xp stays in the ~2.5–38 band, potion/brew xp stays in the existing 25–200 band). Potion brew xp continues the existing 25(attack)→100(super-attack) line up to truechord_draught 200, so a top brew is ~2x a super-attack — reasonable for an L92 multi-stat sink. Herb-seed Farming xp/growTicks extend the existing watermelon (L47, plant 48.5, grow 350) curve: truechord at L85 plants for 110xp over 600 ticks, keeping growth slow enough that herbs stay valuable and the 41-patch cluster doesn't trivialise output (a full cluster cycle still gates on grow time, not walking). Boost magnitudes mirror OSRS-style additive deltas that the combat path already supports via level deltas: regular +3, super +5, extreme +7, multi-stat combos +3/+4 each — strong but transient and consumed, so they're a sink, not power creep, and don't touch permanent gear bonuses. Level gating: herb growing (L24+) sits above the crop tier so Farming has continuous content L1→85; Herblore potions span L12→92 with no >8-level gap, fixing today's L9→L38 dead zone. Verdancourt's high terrace is double-gated (Herblore 82 AND Farming 85) so endgame reagents stay scarce.
postBlurb: VERDANCOURT OPENS ITS GATES — the Greening Garth is found. Where the old gardener tilled two lonely furrows, a whole terraced town now hums to the Greenchord, the buried root-vein the Sustainers have tended in secret. Bring a rake and a dibber: forty-one patches climb three terraces, herb seeds from harralander to the rare truechord bloom now take root in Cantorne soil, and Apothecary Vell's cauldron resolves them into chords you can drink. And drink you should — strength, super-defence, ranging, and the storied Truechord Draught now bite in battle as sharply as any blade. But beware the Blight souring the upper rows; the Offnote covets a sour note in every garden. Plant true. Brew true. Stay in tune.

verdict.mechanicFit:
Most mechanics are pure-data and fit existing systems; TWO require new engine code, and the spec correctly flags both.

PURE DATA (no engine change):
- Herb growing: append to recipes.json.seeds (schema {seed,produce,level,plantXp,harvestXp,growTicks} confirmed at intent-produce.ts:59/331). farm-rake/plant/harvest loop validates Farming level + consumes seed + grants produce already. Works as-is.
- Herb cleaning: append to recipes.json.herbs (schema {grimy,clean,level,xp}). Indexed as 'clean|<clean>' and run through the generic `make` intent (intents.ts:754). No station required (Herblore -> stationReq null). Works.
- Standard potions (vial+herb+secondary): append to recipes.json.potions (schema {output,level,xp,herb,secondary}). Built via 'potion|<output>' make recipe, inputs HARDCODED as vial_of_water+herb+secondary (intents.ts:758). Brewing is stationless today — spec's recommendation to keep it stationless is correct; brew_cauldron is optional decoration.
- Gather objects (limpwurt_plant/white_berry_bush/snape_grass): need (a) an object def in objects.json `objs`, (b) a GATHER_REQS entry {skill:'Farming'} (intents.ts:694), AND CRITICALLY (c) a SKILL_OBJS/`skillObjs` entry with {level,xp,item,depleteChance,respawn,lowRate,highRate} — the gather intent reads its data from SKILL_OBJS[type] (intents.ts:289), not from GATHER_REQS. Spec under-specifies this: it mentions GATHER_REQS + object def but NOT the required skillObjs data block. Still data-only, no code change. 'Farming' is a valid SkillName (state.ts:90).
- NPCs/shops: pure data (npcs.json, shops.json).

NEW ENGINE CODE REQUIRED (2 items):
1. consume/boost hook: today the `consume` intent (intent-produce.ts:684) only honours `restoresPrayer` data-driven; boost/heal/restoreStats/restoreEnergy do NOT exist. Adding stat boosts requires: a new transient state field (e.g. statBoosts:[{skill,amount,until}]), threading it through combat's lvl()/skillLevel feed (combat.ts:159-173 derive eff/maxHit from lvl(state,'Attack'/'Strength') server-side), and a tick-based expiry. This is genuinely new combat-adjacent code, NOT pure data — spec admits this is 'the one real addition'. restoreStats/restoreEnergy similarly need new state plumbing. DRINKABLE allow-list (intent-produce.ts:683) must be expanded to the new potion ids or they return 'not drinkable'.
2. Extreme-tier potions (super_attack+resonant_dust, etc.): the 'potion|' recipe inputs are HARDCODED to vial_of_water+herb+secondary (intents.ts:758). A potion whose input is a finished super-potion + resonant_dust (no vial) does NOT fit. Requires generalizing potion inputs to arbitrary inputs[] OR a new recipe table — NEW code. Spec flags this.
3. farm-compost / farm-apply-compost / compost_bin: entirely new intents + new FarmPatch.compost state field + harvest-yield logic in farm-harvest (intent-produce.ts:351). New engine code; spec flags it. compost_bin also a new object.
balanceFlags: Existing potion xp curve: attack_potion 25xp@L3 -> super_attack 100xp@L45. Spec's potion xp (strength 50@L12 ... truechord_draught 200@L92) is monotonic and in-band — sane.

Existing clean-herb xp: 2.5@L3 -> 8.8@L40. Spec jumps to 27.5@L20 for grimy_harralander clean, rising to 86@L82. This is a STEEP discontinuity vs the existing irit 8.8@L40, but matches OSRS real curve and is plausible for higher tiers; flag for intentional review — clean-herb xp roughly 3x the existing top entry at a lower level (27.5@L20 vs 8.8@L40).
Existing seed plantXp: 8@L1 -> 48.5@L47. Spec truechord_seed plant 110@L85 / harvest 50 is a large extrapolation but monotonic; acceptable.

Value curve sane: existing eye_of_newt 3, ranarr_weed 40, super_attack 90, prayer_potion 120. Spec values (grimy 18->140, clean 30->240, potions 30->500, reward gear 2000-4500) extend smoothly. truechord_draught 500 and resonant_dibber 4500 are the new ceiling — reasonable for L92/L70 top-tier.
prayer_renewal restores 35 vs existing prayer_potion restoresPrayer:25 — consistent upgrade, fits the restoresPrayer data path with no new code (only DRINKABLE allow-list add).

energy_potion 'secondary snape_grass' but recipes line says 'harralander + chocolate-free; secondary snape_grass' — ambiguous/garbled secondary; integrator must pin secondary = snape_grass for the 3-input potion schema.
verdict.notes:
No id collisions: none of the 50+ proposed item ids, nor the object types (compost_bin, limpwurt_plant, white_berry_bush, snape_grass, brew_cauldron, and the herb-patch reuse of farming_patch), nor the 5 NPC ids exist today. The intended-existing references all resolve correctly: eye_of_newt, super_attack, ranarr_weed, vial_of_water, prayer_potion, grimy_ranarr, guam_leaf all exist in items.json. NOTE: object type 'snape_grass' as a gather object collides conceptually with item id 'snape_grass' — they live in different namespaces (objects.json.objs vs items.json) so it is technically fine, but recommend distinct ids (e.g. object 'snape_grass_clump') to avoid confusion.

Required corrections for the integrator:
1. Gather objects MUST also get a `skillObjs` data block in objects.json ({level,xp,item,depleteChance,respawn,lowRate,highRate}) — the gather intent reads SKILL_OBJS[type] for everything (intents.ts:289), GATHER_REQS only supplies skill/tool. The spec omits the skillObjs block.
2. Expand DRINKABLE set (intent-produce.ts:683) to every new consumable potion id or consume returns 'not drinkable'.
3. Extreme potions need generalized recipe inputs (the 'potion|' make recipe hardcodes vial+herb+secondary at intents.ts:758) — add an inputs[] table; do NOT shoehorn into the existing herb/secondary fields.
4. The stat-boost system is the only substantive engine addition: new transient state.statBoosts, integration into combat.ts lvl() feed (lines 159-173) and a tick expiry. restoreStats/restoreEnergy/heal-on-drink also need new plumbing beyond the restoresPrayer precedent.
5. compost system (farm-compost intent, farm-apply-compost / farm-plant payload extension, FarmPatch.compost field, harvest-yield branch in farm-harvest) is new code — currently farm-harvest yields randInt(3,5) with no compost path.
6. Fix the garbled energy_potion secondary (pin to snape_grass).
7. Herb seeds produce the GRIMY herb on harvest (correct in spec) and randInt(3,5) yield is consistent with existing crops — no engine change for growing.
